summary: Recent updates to the negotiation engine, which is part of the JSEP (JavaScript Session Establishment Protocol) used for handling media sessions like video calls, aim to improve its efficiency and performance. The changes include stopping the use of a process that was not suitable for certain application scenarios and refining when transceivers—components that manage media streams—are associated based on the state of the remote media section. These adjustments are significant for enhancing the reliability and responsiveness of real-time communications, ensuring a smoother experience in applications relying on technologies like WebRTC, the backbone of video calling. **Overall, these tweaks stand to boost user experience by making media connections more streamlined and effective.**
